editableNewDialogue.text: I’m formatting a Cantonese resume. Should I put dates as YYYY年MM月, or is YYYY/MM fine?
Both are acceptable. For a more formal, local style, use「YYYY年MM月」(e.g.,「2024年9月–2025年1月」). If you want a clean, compact look,「YYYY/MM」is also common—just keep the format consistent throughout.
If I don’t know the exact day, is it okay to write only the month?
Yes. Month-level dates are normal on resumes. You can write「2025年1月–2025年6月」or「2025/01–2025/06」. Avoid adding a day if you’re unsure.
Do I need a summary at the top, or can I skip it?
You can skip it if the resume is short. If you include one, keep it to 1–2 lines focusing on role-fit and key strengths. Example (generic):「具前線客戶服務經驗，做事細心守時，能配合輪班並與團隊合作。」
Random question: why do some job ads ask for a “CV” and others say “resume”?

Often it’s regional convention. In many places, “resume” implies a 1–2 page, role-targeted summary, while “CV” can mean a longer, more comprehensive document (common in academia). Some employers use the terms interchangeably in job ads.
我想喺份CV入面加多句，平時返學做project嗰陣我多數都會負責整PPT同埋上台講解。

可以呀，你可以喺「技能」或者「相關經驗」加一句，例如：
- 熟悉製作簡報（PPT），並具備上台匯報及講解能力
我想更正返：其實我唔係玩欖球（Rugby），我係想加「攔網球」做興趣，之前我打錯咗。
明白，咁我幫你喺「興趣」入面將「欖球」改做「攔網球」。
